IUU Vsl List: Date included in the IUU Vessel List: 02 March 2026
Position: Eastern quadrant of the SPRFMO Convention Area (various positions)
Activities: SPRFMO CMM 12 requires that the at-sea transfer of fuel, crew, gear or any other supplies between two vessels in the Convention Area shall only be undertaken between authorised vessels included in the Commission Record of Vessels. The bunkering vessel, FENG HAI, was flagged to Sierra Leone (SL) in March 2025. SL does not have Member or CNCP status in SPRFMO and its vessels are not found on the SPRFMO Record of Vessels. The vessel FENG HAI (SL) was previously authorised on the SPRFMO Record of Vessels, and operated in SPRFMO, as the Belize flagged vessel XIANG HAI (but was removed from the Belize Registry in October 2024). Following the end of its SPRFMO authorisation the bunkering vessel serviced SPRFMO fishing vessels during November 2024. In 2025, the vessel returned to the SPRFMO Convention area as the renamed/reflagged vessel FENG HAI (SL) and provided bunkering services to SPRFMO fishing vessels in the Convention Area during May - July 2025 without being authorised on the SPRFMO Record of Vessels.
Actions taken: Sierra Leone Maritime Administration: Notified the Secretariat on 2 Oct 2025 that the bunkering of fishing vessels was prohibited and that the FENG HAI had been ex officio cancelled from its registry. Sierra Leone - Ex Officio Cancellation of Registry Letter - Actions taken: 1) Ex officio cancelled from the Registry of the Republic of Sierra Leone; 2) All certificates issued on behalf of the Flag State cease to be valid; 3) The Owner has been fined (and paid) five thousand US dollars (USD $5,000); 4) The Ex Officio Cancellation of Registry enters into force as of the 30th of September 2025; and 5) The obligation remains with the Owner to apply for the “de-registration certificate” (Certificate issued on 22 December 2025 at 08:35 UTC).
Outcome: Placed on the Final IUU Vessel List
